title: Transforming Challenging Lips: How to Blend Previous Treatments for Natural Results.
filename: Transforming Challenging Lips： How to Blend Previous Treatments for Natural Results..en.vtt
excerpt: This patient's lip was tricky she'd been previously treated and what I was trying to do was actually blend in some of the treatment from a previous practitioner so she has rather dominant lateral Lips This is a practitioner I suspect who's just done a stock treatment on each pati…

segments.text: this patient's lip was tricky she'd been previously treated and what I was trying to do was actually blend in some of the treatment from a previous practitioner so she has rather dominant lateral Lips This is a practitioner I suspect who's just done a stock treatment on each patient she really needs nothing laterally and I think the lateral treatment unless potentially it's migrated has effectively added to a sense of sadness or negativity to her lateral oral aspect so what I tried to do is blend that in you'll see me in underneath the lip in the white of the lip which is very unusual but I think it made a good difference in terms of supporting the lower lip and then most of my effort then goes to treating the middle third of the lip because really what I want is the middle lip to dominate the lateral just like we do with chins dominating jows I'm trying to create the same effect here wasn't straightforward I think it made a good Improvement and there's lots to learn about the battles you will go through as a practitioner particularly when you're treating patients who've had many treatments elsewhere this little Shadow here is part of

a complex that probably allows this to be more rotated outwards a little Shadow so if I may be able to actually help a little bit by just treating this little area of volume loss that might support the lateral lip need a little Bend in my needle to do it [Music] I think it the very least this decreases the boundary the what's defining the dominance of the lateral lip it's not necessarily lifting it but it's hiding the uh it's making it less dominant so visually it's doing a job even if mechanically it's not rotating it

so I'm just noticing as I'm gearing up towards the lips it looks a little bit Fuller on the left side than the right this is actually good because I think if I can make the right side look like the left closer to it this particularly in this cubicle here that will actually make your lips look more beautiful so I want to try just a little bit there happily the same benefit will occur from the side if I can get the lower lip to project outward slightly it'll bring Harmony to the top lip which is well projected lower lip angle runs down the filum usually to an angle on the chin so ideally it would be here you can see it there that's what I'm going to try and create [Music]

two boluses on top of each other see the product spread slightly which means I need to do this key shaping element it's usually where it comes together is when you basically bring the filler up [Music] little bit of bleeding I'm going to stop that [Music]

[Music] how important is that massage that you do you know when you inject a lip with filler does it actually move around you know when you put it in yeah uh I think it's you can squeeze it into the right place the connective tissue that makes it hold its shape better so um at Le sometimes you put it in and it actually doesn't seem to do anything and you squeeze it and then it

transcript: this patient's lip was tricky she'd been previously treated and what I was trying to do was actually blend in some of the treatment from a previous practitioner so she has rather dominant lateral Lips This is a practitioner I suspect who's just done a stock treatment on each patient she really needs nothing laterally and I think the lateral treatment unless potentially it's migrated has effectively added to a sense of sadness or negativity to her lateral oral aspect so what I tried to do is blend that in you'll see me in underneath the lip in the white of the lip which is very unusual but I think it made a good difference in terms of supporting the lower lip and then most of my effort then goes to treating the middle third of the lip because really what I want is the middle lip to dominate the lateral just like we do with chins dominating jows I'm trying to create the same effect here wasn't straightforward I think it made a good Improvement and there's lots to learn about the battles you will go through as a practitioner particularly when you're treating patients who've had many treatments elsewhere this little Shadow here is part of a complex that probably allows this to be more rotated outwards a little Shadow so if I may be able to actually help a little bit by just treating this little area of volume loss that might support the lateral lip need a little Bend in my needle to do it [Music] I think it the very least this decreases the boundary the what's defining the dominance of the lateral lip it's not necessarily lifting it but it's hiding the uh it's making it less dominant so visually it's doing a job even if mechanically it's not rotating it so I'm just noticing as I'm gearing up towards the lips it looks a little bit Fuller on the left side than the right this is actually good because I think if I can make the right side look like the left closer to it this particularly in this cubicle here that will actually make your lips look more beautiful so I want to try just a little bit there happily the same benefit will occur from the side if I can get the lower lip to project outward slightly it'll bring Harmony to the top lip which is well projected lower lip angle runs down the filum usually to an angle on the chin so ideally it would be here you can see it there that's what I'm going to try and create [Music] two boluses on top of each other see the product spread slightly which means I need to do this key shaping element it's usually where it comes together is when you basically bring the filler up [Music] little bit of bleeding I'm going to stop that [Music] [Music] how important is that massage that you do you know when you inject a lip with filler does it actually move around you know when you put it in yeah uh I think it's you can squeeze it into the right place the connective tissue that makes it hold its shape better so um at Le sometimes you put it in and it actually doesn't seem to do anything and you squeeze it and then it holds the shape yeah [Music]
